steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: Jack's final remark is sarcastic: he suggests Bartley would carry a “leaky pail on your head in place of a hat, that way you'd not be without some cause of complaining.” The phrase about always having a cause of complaining is the decisive signal of what Jack believes.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: Jack's sarcasm shows he believes Bartley is perpetually dissatisfied and will always find something to complain about, rain or no rain. The other choices either describe things unsupported by the text (A, B) or mistake what Jack is actually criticizing (C). That matches choice D — D is correct.
